description: You are a production accountant on an independent film (budget ~$1.8M independent feature). Microsoft Excel is open with film_budget.xlsx. The 'Line_Item_Detail' sheet has 32 line items across 4 budget categories (Above-the-Line, Below-the-Line Production, Post-Production, Other) with columns: Account_Code, Category, Department, Line_Item, Budget_Amount, Actual_Amount, PO_Committed, Invoice_Paid, Source_Reference. Complete the 'Budget_vs_Actual' sheet: for each of the 4 categories, calculate totals using SUMIF against the Category column — Total Budget, Total Actual, Variance (Actual minus Budget), Variance Percentage ((Actual-Budget)/Budget×100), Total Committed (PO_Committed), Total Paid (Invoice_Paid), Remaining Committed (Committed minus Paid), EAC (Estimate at Completion, which equals Actual for a completed production), ETC (Estimate to Complete = EAC minus Paid), and Status_Flag ('OVER_BUDGET' if Variance_Pct > 5%, 'ON_TRACK' if between -5% and 5%, 'UNDER_BUDGET' if < -5%). Fill the GRAND TOTAL row. Complete the 'Department_Summary' sheet: for each of the ~20 departments, calculate budget and actual totals using SUMIFS, variance, count of line items (COUNTIF), and identify the largest cost overrun item and its dollar amount using INDEX/MATCH with MAX. Complete the 'Contingency_Tracker' sheet: calculate the original budget excluding the contingency line item, the contingency amount ($140,000), total actual spend, total overrun (actual minus original budget if positive), contingency used (equals overrun), contingency remaining, utilization percentage, projected final cost, and Budget_Health flag ('CRITICAL' if utilization > 80%, 'WARNING' if > 50%, 'HEALTHY' otherwise). Save as C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\ExcelTasks\film_budget.xlsx using Ctrl+S.

metadata.industry: Arts, Entertainment, and Media / Film Production
metadata.data_source: Budget structure based on AICP standardized bid form; crew rates from BLS OEWS May 2023 wage data and DGA/SAG-AFTRA/IATSE/WGA 2023 published rate schedules; contingency reserve per completion bond requirements
